channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio… — 55 replies — 220.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio…

Open thread • Replies: 55 • Updated: 2026-05-16 12:37 UTC

OP

Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main question is at the bottom below the dashed line, so you can skip all of this text if you want.

Is anyone here good with statistics or math? Idk the correct field that would apply here, some math shit.
I’ve been working on a strategy for years at this point, but I’ve been focused on a single thing for about the last half year. I’ve back tested it till 2020 and its results are the photo. I wanted to use it with prop firms to make some cash since the potential profit with them is way higher than using my own cash, but the drawdowns are too severe. And prop firms are hella strict on their drawdowns.
I’ve run the math and if I scale down enough to where I can trade without blowing up my account, at the rate of withdrawals and taxes and splits, I’d be working for nothing. Min wage basically. And if I scale up then I just blow up accounts due to drawdown. I don’t care about profit either. I need smoothness. Even if I take a major hit in points, so long as it becomes smoother that’s still fine since I can just scale up on prop firms.

It’s basic metrics right now are
WR: 53.78%
Profit Factor: 1.36
Trades Per Day: 8.33

There’s a lot more data but that’s the gist.
The strategy is a blend so it’s RR varies, but it’s pretty close to 1:1RR

—————————-
Here’s my main question: the strategy currently averages 8 trades a day. That’s a lot. Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right? Surely. I’ve tried a lot of things but I’ve failed all analytical approaches. I’ve done a lot of digging and I think I’m just out of my element here.

I think my request is gonna fall on deaf ears but I’m hopeful. Maybe someone earnest and smarter than me can help.

Early replies (first 3)

62220033
BUmp before I sleep

62220056
buy an ad grok

62222767
>>62220056
Does my post sound like an Ai made it lol? It’s a Genuine question

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62228111
>>62228084
It feels good to be doubted because what you've done amazes someone. Thanks for giving me the inspiration to keep at it.

62228122
>>62228083
Okay, here's my conundrum then: Suppose you have a list of 10K+ trades spanning from 2020 until now. Each trade has its data attached: date, entry, PnL, and any other metrics that might be important.

How would you go about finding some method or filter to cut out the low-quality trades from the high-quality trades? Ultimately, my strategy relies on momentum, so days where price chops heavily hurt me badly.

Perhaps I could find a way to either identify a chop day before it happens, or find a new addition or advanced criteria that would allow me to dynamically adjust my profit target and stop-loss based on what the model forecasts for that day.

Those are two ideas I have, but I haven’t been able to implement them simply because I’m not sure how to go about it.

I’ve checked the data and found that even the losing trades often have some degree of favorable movement before they eventually lose. That makes me wonder if I could find something that would tell me to reduce my profit target size for that day based on XYZ criteria.

Again, I’m kind of just spitballing because I genuinely don’t know where else to go with this. Everything I’ve tried to improve win rate by cutting losses has inevitably cut out winners as well. So I’m not sure what the right approach is.

62228424
bump

62228603
>>62228122
Low quality and high quality? That’s any MFT Strat lol

62228693
>>62228603
also if i help i get nothing from this

62228705
>>62228603
the strategy is not anything that would require low latency at least to my knowledge since the avg trade lasts anywhere from half an hour to several hours. again, i got no idea where to go from here. any tips would be appreciated

>>62228693
well if you helped me improve it, you could do whatever you want with it. It's not some proprietary strategy that requires some exotic data, it's pretty simple and generic. maybe that's why it's had a minor edge for so long. I would only be trading on it using prop firms, their sim accounts don't trade on live markets. and even if i did, i'm only trading NQ which is hella liquid. And as I've mentioned before, once I make a certain amount of money I'm done for good. Never gonna trade again. In theory if I could get this thing to a workable state, scaling it up would take a few months and I'd hit my goal within a year or two.

62228733
>>62224557
Depends on capital restrictions, an arbitrage or HFT strategy can easily get 1%/day but probably has limits on how much capital it can grow into.

62228952
>>62228733
lol

62228956
add 5% annual IBKR fees itll auto divide for margin or funding then 10 ticks of slippage

62229415
I still have yet to see someone who can help.

>>62228733
1% a day seems impossible in the long run. No strategy can have only good days with no bad days.

>>62228956
If they are buying and selling contracts to such an extent then fees would def eat into it. But if it’s a single buy or sell, or a position that’s held for hours then I don’t see why not.

62229502
Bump

62230495
>>62229415
I coded up orb it doesnt beat fees the original paper is a meme in the algorithmic trading community

try on TQQQ

62230578
>>62230495
if you want to talk serious share discord username

62230627
Based OP, a genuinely original effort thread with potential. Will continue to lurk for interest and hopes to obtain some knowledge, but this is out of scope

62232509
>>62230495
It had basis on ORB and VWAP but isn't just that.

>>62230578
Before any of that I'd like to hear your ideas on what you'd do / suggest for me to improve the strategy. I've already tried a lot of things, so I'd like to know how you could help me.

>>62230627
Thanks, but I haven't really gotten anything good out of this thread yet hahaha

62232797
bump

>>62230627
Also I thought about this a bit more, edge erosion is a real thing. The ironic thing is that if I released it with the intention of helping others, some actual firm might come along and pick that up and run with it. I recall there was this one strategy I was researching a while back. It performed well from 2016 all the way until 2020. But after 2020 till now it started performing worse and worse and worse. Until 2025 and 2026 where the returns were so ass that the only way you'd profit off of it was to essentially scalp it down to the ticks. Only a group with low latency and a huge bankroll would profit off of that. I saw live how a strategy had it's edge eroded.

I'm not claiming what I have is something that amazing. but that is a worry. At the most I'd share it with close friends and family and help them succeed. After all, what is the point of wealth if not to help others.

62233623
Bump

62234201
Bumpinonce more before I sleep

62236353
bump once more. is there one out here who's got the smarts?

62237934
>>62236353
all im going to say is your approach is too simple i thought orb had alpha decay years ago lol

62238015
>>62237934
Anon, I'd like some constructive feedback. Regardless of whether the approach is simple or not, the fact of the matter is that the data backs it up. Only one of the lines uses ORB.

The full blend, as shown in the first picture, is split across 5 different lines. The correlation between them, while not perfectly non-overlapping, is still low enough that it’s rare for them all to lose on the same day.

In the days since I made this, I was actually able to find a way to further cut down a few outliers, reducing total trades taken by 16% while increasing points by about 9%.

But these are just marginal improvements, and I’m hesitant to keep chasing them due to fears of overfitting.

My hypothesis is that, with how many trades I take, I should be able to quantitatively chop down the worst of them. I’ve already managed to do that with relatively simple filters over the past few days, but I’m running up against a wall, so I want to avoid going too far. THAT's why I made this thread. In hopes of finding someone with knowledge.

62238067
>>62238015
ok, now we're getting somewhere. you are afraid of overfitting and not even using ML lmfao noob

62238083
>>62238067
Rather than implement something I have no understanding of, I'm sticking to what I do know. I'll say it again. if you wanna offer criticism or advice, keep it constructive. just saying "ML" is so vague. Be more specific. ML in what way?

62238370
>>62238083
any sort of model.predict() is going to overfit unless you train on like 20-30% of the data thats implicitly going to be trained or predicted on, so with pure ta or price action strategies overfitting is never possible since theyre raw calculations of mathematics on price action time series data


holy shit anon you should know this im sharing liquid gold intuition with you

62238387
>>62219625
That equity curve is either a data issue or clearly just picking pennies up infront of a steamroller.
You being obssessed with "smoothness" is also a red flag.
You thinking "the data backs it up" with confidence is also a BIG red flag, this is not how real trading works.
Anyone can just run some lookahead biased regressions and uncover a "strategy", anything that is actually statistically robust and ACTIONABLE will be based off of forward walks and have effect sizes so small you'll be questioning whether its even real, and only THEN have you found something that might actually indeed be real.

62238401
>>62238370
I tried a variety of strategies based on price action but I could never get any of them to work. This setup is the best I could get. I tried other things but they wouldn’t work. I’m still not understanding how this ML would filter out losses in a way that’s actionable. If it just gives me some highly specific bucket like “trades between 0.75% and 0.84% of OR width are losing” then that’s just over fitting.

>>62238387
I’ve mentioned why. It’s for the sake of prop firms. They have hard drawdown rules. As mentioned before, i can theoretically pass and even get on a funded account with the strat as it is now. But it takes too long to pass and too long to get a payout. Most of what I read in ur response sounds like fluff no offense. I’m all for constructive criticism but I don’t see how any of what you said applies to my.

My strategy is a blend of 5 simple ideas that each trade on their own with the idea of:

Rather than find one single strategy that is good and trades often,
Find multiple good strategies that don’t trade often individually and then use them together.

I haven’t done any look back biased regressions, I have a separate 2026 only sample that I’ll test my strategies on to make sure they’re not overfit. And they’ll work just fine on new never before seen data.

Guys I am open to any constructive criticism, just please make it make sense in the context of what I’m asking.

62238500
>>62238401
Listen bro you don't just throw a couple of funny numbers in a computer and cash comes out thats just not how the world works.
You believe you need to be super opaque so we don't "steal" your strategy, but there are thousands of guys like you who think they have some top secret "strat" that they just need someone to fund them for.
What model are you even using? Some GARCH? HMM? Frequency of your data?

62238660
> top secret strategy trades the Nasdaq, an index that only goes up
> it makes money
> anon thinks he's a genius
> loses to buy-and-hold TQQQ

62238773
>>62238500
I’m not being opaque. I said I want advice from someone who has knowledge in fields related to statistics and quantitative analysis. Do you?

No, I don’t use GARCH. My strategy does not tune itself, it does not use some regressive or adjusting method. It follows the same rules trading in 2021 as it does in 2025 in the data set. The TP and SL’s are the only dynamic variable because they are based on VWAP, or ORB, or the volume profile as well as fixed % based on the open market value. A 20 point move in 2020 is far different than a 20 point move in 2025.
But they are not hand tuned or adjusted by me. It sets the TP based on the given range. It’s as simple as that.

I only used volume and OHLC of NQ and ES via 1m candles.

Also who the hell said I need funding? You should read all of my messages before making assumptions.

>>62238660
The worst drawdowns were approximately 3,000 points, while peak equity was around 50,000 points.

This is just napkin math, but let’s make a few simplifying assumptions:

Assume 1 NQ contract per trade. Some lines had multiple take-profits, but we’ll simplify. NQ is $20 per point, so a 3,000-point drawdown would require $60,000 in capital to withstand the worst observed drawdown. To be more conservative, let’s round that up to $100,000 of starting cash. Unexessary but let’s do it anyways.

Starting with $100,000, if running equity reaches 50,000 points, that’s 50,000 × $20 = $1,000,000.

So, theoretically, starting with $100,000 and ending around $1 million is possible under these assumptions.

TQQQ went up about 230% from 2020 till 2025, where my data ends.

230% vs 900%.

If I had the money, I’d just put it all into SCHD and chill. But I don’t. That’s why I made this thread. That’s why I’m making this strategy and asking if anyone here can offer ideas that make sense in the context of what I’m asking.

62238820
>>62238773
duh you don't hold TQQQ through 2022 retard
I already understand you don't want to apply any brains but jesus

item.title: how to save ICP? — 27 replies — 48.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
how to save ICP?

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-05-16 12:05 UTC

OP

for more context, i'm a solo motoko dev. i want more eyes on ICP so our bags can pump.

i'm thinking a canister that can host censorable thing and will make a lot of people mad & outraged. i plan to share the canister on /pol/, but what should i host? JQ is mainstream now so i doubt anyone would care.

or do you anons have any other idea? anyway, i'm just tired of holding for 5 years. my final option is to wait for toko to launch then do an nft collection there... ugh...

Early replies (first 3)

62230305
>>62230261
Badlands, GPU Subnets, a PoW shitcoin (like BOB) that actually moons and doesnt dump below 100m mcap, blacklisting the entire third world.

Too bad none of these will ever happen

62230390
>>62230305
>PoW
what's stopping anyone from creating a new pow coin on icp?

62230410
>>62230261
Maybe not make it an uncapped VC scam token? Idk might be too much to ask of Dominic. Also raj is a scammer fuck icp

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62230416
>>62230261
There is no "censored-thing hosting" on ICP, you can't even become a host without approval. It will be removed immediately.

62230547
>>62230261
Well done, my good and faithful servant! Thank you for playing! Keep writing Motoko applications, maybe that will pay off some day (for me, of course -- between my alimony, child support, and life in Switzerland, I'm not cheap!)

>>62230390
It's called BOB and it caused the highest CBR of 2.1T cycles/s back in September 2024. Its site bob.fun died last year, but bobpad is around here: https://cjxxj-paaaa-aaaas-amqsq-cai.icp0.io/ -- not sure if this is even related to the original coin

62230696
>>62230390
nothing. keeping the third world away from the coin is the hard part. Nobody wants to buy third world bags and third world baggies cant hodl

62231036
>>62230696
ICP is so incredibly mismanaged and Euro liberalism coded. They naively gave away billions via ICP Hubs programs and think that partnering with shithole states like Pakistan is the key to success. The tech itself is not bad; my grievances are with management. Of course now that Jan left, all that's left now is to milk it for all it's worth. Mission70 was the first step for that, to fuck over investors with the most conviction.

There is a 2-year countdown timer somewhere to implosion, because a huge chunk of the maxed out neurons started dissolving to have a chance to exit before the price finally goes to $0.03

62231086
>>62230261
My idea is a perp dex like hype but running entirely on ICP

62231139
>>62230261
So what was that pump to $4 all about?

62231161
>>62230261
>ICP
>motoko
>NFT
>5 years holding a shitcoin

LMFAO it's 2026 baggie, you LOST, just sell or hold that shit to ZERO and stop posting anything about this dead blockchain, nigger.

62231224
/pol/ brainlets btfo, ok? Moonman scam finally getting reveal

62231240
>>62231036
Adoption is accelerating

62231418
>>62230261
be patient

62231523
>>62231036
>, because a huge chunk of the maxed out neurons started dissolving to have a chance to exit before the price finally goes to $0.03

Maybe the death spiral will happen for real this time and I get to write it off on taxes for the next 10 years

62231977
>>62230261
ICP is over.
However, if you really want to salvage it, i would say a killer decentralized board, that would replace 4chan. I mean way better than the current seachan which is pretty awful.

62232778
>>62231418
5 years is not patient enough? dont be an idiot.
there are no signs of adoptions.

62232787
>>62231977
checked. ok, but would you pay to use it? of course it would be extremely cheap: 0.0001 ICP per post or 0.1 ICP per month or 1 ICP per year? (if icp pumps, reprice it to be lower).

benefits: filters most bots, jeets & jews because they wont pay.

62232792
>>62231139
mexc posting infinity logo on an astronaut, farming engagement. many people thought it's about icp until mexc revealed that it's not. the absolute state of icp holders.

62232811
>>62230261
there’s gotta be a good DAO idea that can only be done on ICP. other chains can’t host sites at web speed so they can’t host uber complex daos at web speed. could be a pay to win game with community governance as a focal point. gorillion things you can do with a dao, one idea has gotta stick. my bet would be on a decentralized community wiki.

62233036
This really is a pathetic performance.

62234357
>>62230261
Just put a game on it again

62234551
>>62232787
Pay for post doesn't work. dchan already tried and failed and it wasn't even paying but transaction fee for the post. The problem is not even paying, it's that most will not be motivated to buy ICP and log in with a wallet. Strong captcha to filter.

62234606
>>62230261
>down 99.6% from ATH

lol, lmao even.

62234959
>>62234551
i see.. you're actually right. i have to research what would make people want to dload a wallet & buy icp to spend it.

62235007
I have millions of ICP and will continue giga dumping my bags on every pump. Be warned.

62235111
>>62234606
>lol, lmao even.

yeah that crap will never recover. it was just another jeet rugpull scam!

62238778
they've removed the Grant section from dfinity website
it's over isnt it?

item.title: Kraken has moved to CCIP. — 59 replies — 46.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Kraken has moved to CCIP.

Open thread • Replies: 59 • Updated: 2026-05-16 11:22 UTC

OP

kek fuddies

Early replies (first 3)

62230910
>>62230891
Time to dump btc from 81 and eth from 2300

62230914
WAGMI WAGMI

62230921
>shovel salesman announces that shovels are useful, actually

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62231853
>>62230891
>>62231282

Lido too now lol
Fresh off the presses

62231868
>>62231843
It was foretold. Obvious in hindsight.

62231901
cuckolds are embarassing lmao

price + chart

62231946
>>62231853
ALL AT ONCE

62231990
>>62231853
oooooooof

62231992
>>62231946
OLD NEWS buddy

62232282
>>62230891

This is actually FUD. Cross Chain Interoperability Protocol is a load of gobbly gook (I skimmed thoroughly) and chainlink still hasn't proven its original use case (solving the problem of getting data into smart contracts in a decentralized manner).

The very concept of Sergey's oracle system is unproven, although sensible. All this bullshit being put on top is not "exciting" or "value-added," it is FUD. It is a stream of bullshit without any actual real world uses.

You retards are getting excited by someone scooping shit on top of technological marvels. Imagine the first airplane, thats a blockchain. Now imagine a woman jumping on top the cockpit and taking a huge dump. Thats what your getting excited about.

62232408
>>62231646
Saar I'm sooo white saar!

62232418
Wait I thought LINK was a SCAM?!? You're telling me the most upstanding, honest, and forthcoming company in crypto, King Kraken, has literally entrusted their reputation in the hands of a fat russian big mac enjoyer? So LINK is not a scam? Somebody comfort me please...

62232437
>>62232418
the scoundrel sergey is very convincing

62232441
>>62230891
Thats cool and all but.... one small problem.... uhh... price??? Oh...... and... chart??

62232461
>>62232408
that's adem newfaggot
lurk moar

62232525
>>62232282
This so much, all the top financial institutions of the world are getting absolutely gobblecocked by sergey

Now X R P. THAT is the one correct thing they are choosing!!

62236073
>>62231115
Hello mentally ill faggot how are you doing today?

62236163
>>62231853
This is old news isn't it? Lido was already with CCIP. They're just now publishing their rationale behind the decision.

62236196
>>62236073
doing great thanks
oh btw: pee plus see, bitch

62236449
>>62231646
>i have already won at life

yeah for a guy that's "winning" you don't seem very happy about that

>>62231646
>hope you fixed your porn/masturbation addiction though

you're addicted to keeping track of a random online personality jacking off
dude that's fucking wild

have you told anyone irl?

62236468
>>62236449
Hope you're doing well, Adem

62236472
hey guys --- how's the new "integration" going today? lets check the numbers shall we?


>CCIP fees last 24h: $637

bahahahaha

>Link price action last 24h: -5.2%

bahahahahahahahaah holy shit


nobody, and i mean NOBODY, gets viciously c u c k h o l d e d quite like the boyscouts.... and then comes back time and time again, absolutely BEGGING for more

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62236479
>>62230910
Oh my fuckk you weren't even lying.

I hate this gay ass market.

62236498
>>62231117
You know he probably owns more Link than almost anyone else here, hes just be a dickhole for fun because he knows whats coming.

62236508
>>62230891
i saw kraken now allows margin gambling without having to have a huge portfolio. pretty cool imo. as far as link, i don't think about it at all

62236594
>>62236163
Well here's a completely new one.

62236605
OMFG WE ARE MOONING!!!!!

62236616
HOLY FUCKING SHIT IT JUST KEEPS GOING!!!!!

62236626
>faggot ass stinkies gloating over nothing
This thread is now the $9 dollar waiting room

62236630
can't wait until the failure of stink is so obvious, even the delulu marines cannot deny it any longer

they will be forced to admit that poverty is their only bedfellow

62237484
>>62236630
the thing is this grift can go on for about 8 more years, the next bull will take link up to 17 and then 4 years until it bottoms to 2.50-5 dollars, then next bull will take link to maybe 8-9 dollars. so its going to be about 12 years until the marines realize they fucked up.


by then people like chainlink god will just point out hurdles that chainlik has had.

chainlink reserves will be dumped and i pray to god that another competitor comes and takes away some of the defi market share chainlink has got.

62238651
>>62237484
>the thing is this grift can go on for about 8 more years
Especially when additional grifters like DTCC, State Street, Fidelity, Swift, ... keep joining in.

62238716
>>62231853
chainlink ccip on non-evm chains too, future proof

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 91 replies — 21.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 91 • Updated: 2026-05-16 08:24 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62230021
>>62219942
What's supposed to be happening in the op pic

62231066
>>62225044
I got jewed of my own dip, gf was home all day yesterday and i didn't buy.
I hate women so much is unbelievable

62231072
>>62229557
Why though anon? It’s not like this is some random vaporware project. They already have partnerships with names like GoMining, Ledger, a16zcrypto and others around the ecosystem.

62231081
>>62230004
Why all in? : (

62231090
>>62229433
Good advice so far.

62232211
>>62222594
There's solution for this

62233500
>>62231072
is that legit?

62233508
>>62231066
You hate woman because you like man. Gayish bitcoiner

62233709
>>62233500
Yeah anon. There’s already almost 51k BTC staked in the protocol too, which is like $4B+ worth of BTC already participating around the ecosystem.

62233716
>>62223021
You can always keep slowly adding over time anon. Most people don’t even have close to 1 BTC.

62233736
>>62224655
it will be your opportunity as well.

62233806
>>62229570
I'm from Spain and I live well. Got small biz + doing crypto + forex + stocks. Life is good.

62233821
>>62233709
never though there's protocol exists like this

62233827
>buy btc
buy link
buy ICP
buy avax

62233835
>>62228833
And Ethereum is the godess

62233840
>>62228631
Hes just a homosexual and fears for the virginity of his anus

62233880
>>62233827

worst trash available
>UNGMI

62233986
>>62233821
Yeah anon, it already exists but I think a lot of people just still aren’t paying attention to the newer BTCFi infra yet. And it’s still pretty early too since the TBVs haven’t even launched yet, which should unlock even more native BTC use cases.

62233993
>>62225388
See this anon?

62233997
>>62224679
The funniest part is you probably think debasing fiat printed out of thin air is somehow more “real” than a globally traded asset with trillions in liquidity.

62234000
>>62230021
Gonna be dead broke without BTC lol.

62234021
>>62233806
Aren't you fucked with taxes and DAC8 now, I cannot buy anymore via a cex here

62234099
>>62233840
It sucks being gay

62234109
>>62234021
do the alternative. Privacy

62234248
>>62234021
Government is shit.

62234899
>>62233986
I don’t think that’s enough when ETH literally lives and dies by DeFi activity.

62235825
>>62222594
you don't need to

62237090
>>62220146
give me money

62237423
>>62219995
you're rich mate.

62238430
>>62229077
Exactly, but turning BTC into an active asset will through native staking, instead of just worrying about future risks.

item.title: WILL THIS FUCKING MARKET EVER FUCKING CRASH!!!?!?!??!?!?!??!?!? — 18 replies — 13.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
WILL THIS FUCKING MARKET EVER FUCKING CRASH!!!?!?!??!?!?!??!?!?

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-16 11:20 UTC

OP

WILL THIS FUCKING MARKET EVER FUCKING CRASH!!!?!?!??!?!?!??!?!?

Early replies (first 3)

62233535
Something tells me it'll never crash again (nominally)

62233584
>>62233522
The so called "crash" will recover in 2 months lol

62233643
>>62233522
it's a self-reinforcing bull run. it won't crash ever, it just goes up parabolcally forever due to recursively compounding leverage

item.title: Australia is fucked — 58 replies — 10.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Australia is fucked

Open thread • Replies: 58 • Updated: 2026-05-16 06:55 UTC

OP

>CGT discount - DENIED
>Negative Gearing - DENIED
Where were you when the Australian dream died?

Early replies (first 3)

62224090
have the liberals said they will reverse it? do we just hold and wait?

62224122
>>62223850
Doesn’t this come into effect in 2027? Should I sell all my crypto before 2027?

62224148
>>62223850
its tiring living here
the new rules favour low return long duration assets. high growth assets like btc and leveraged etfs get smoked compared old rules. run a few tests... under 12 months nothing changes.
i wonder if it actually encourages speculation. might as well punt on options or asx bets miners or U.S letfs... you need BIG wins now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62233618
>>62223850
Feeling glad that I moved to the US desu

62233642
>>62233595
CGT change still applies + no passive income + risk(ier)
Rent is going to go up and existing properties may get cheaper as capital moves to new builds, which will only be temporary before investors realise no amount of tax incentives will make new builds a good investment
All that being said, I think Labor loses the election after next (which they'll win) and some of these changes will get reversed

62233646
>>62233642
>expecting liberals to do anything useful ever
lol

62233658
>>62233646
If there's one thing they'll do it's change the CGT tax because it's a layup
Also no one is mentioning that Labor blew past their immigration target for the 3rd year in a row, we have at least 6 more years of infinity Indians pumping up rents

62233662
>>62223850
>>62224281
It’s actually worse than that.
No negative gearing for new buyers, but all the existing owners still get their negative gearing grandfathered in lol.

62233868
>>62224712
You'll get the same discount regardless of when you sell. The new system only applies for gains accrued after 1 July 2027. Which also means that for anything that you sell in the future you'll need to know the price on exactly that date, which will be "lots of fun" for illiquid assets like real estate and artwork.

Personally, if LINK seems to be heading upwards by then, I'll pay CGT on the existing amount and move to Singapore for a while.

>>62226023
My last car (Falcon G6E Turbo) beat the stock market. The poor kid who bought it put himself into ridiculous debt for it.

62234274
>>62233642
All i know is i own a house and investing in non pixelated assets for retirement is still a better option than leaving money in the bank.

62234315
>>62223850
I voted for this

62234422
>>62230860
To where? Everywhere is fucked.

t. Bong

62237110
>>62224122
I thought the discount only affected real estate investments? Does it include any investments including crypto?

From what I've heard it isn't happening until 2027. Gives me time to renovate and sell one of my apartments.

62237297
I'm looking to buy an apartment now. Should I max everything and buy a unit near the cbd for 900k or get a tiny studio for like 600k? Surely not all the new builds are bad?

How do I even save money when shares are fucked? Might as well PPOR MAXX right?

>>62237110
>Gives me time to renovate and sell one of my apartments.
You and everyone else mate. Let me know when you are selling so I know when the bottom is in.

62237384
>>62237110
Everything

62237449
>>62230860
Singapore, I moved to singapore. No capital gains tax LOL richer than Aus LOL got PR LOL WHAT WERE THEY THINKING AAH. But you habe to pay cpf! You can take ypur cpf with ya when you leave

62237658
>>62224281
30% min tax on all capital gains.
Which is retarded some bum makes 100 bucks and has to give 30% away lmao. Should just go into income brackets imho
>>62237297
>Might as well PPOR MAXX right?
I keep seeing people say this. How do you make money if you live in your house? You get railed by stamp duty when you sell.

62237662
>>62237658
actual strat is BHP
fully franked dividends are the future

62237863
not selling any of my shares until libs revoke this shit. Luckily im a bogglehead so ill continue as if nothing happened.

62237955
>>62237658
PPoR doesnt pay tax.
You also dont pay stamp duty when you sell.

Is everyone here a bot?

62237958
>>62237955
Strat is to keep upgrading ppor until you sell to retire or fuck off elsewhere

62238012
Why do you retards think boomers using negative gearing to drive up house prices is a good thing?

62238025
>>62237449
>Singapore, I moved to singapore.
A place like this will be very easy to mass surveil with drones and robo-dogs roaming the streets. Best move is to locate to some semi-remote community in Australia near multiple fresh water sources and get ready to plan ahead for bad days. You want space to roam if things go bad. Sure the taxes are humiliation tier but the land mass itself has other strategic uses that might prove more important down the line. You could also do this in the US but Aussie infrastructure is always lagging a few years which is another plus in this context.

Everyone assumes that we're one-and-done with some covid-style power reach event, but there's no way they don't try that again.

62238061
>>62238012
It's not but the current budget lets them continue doing it

>>62238025
Larp

62238068
>>62238061
Larp insofar as I haven't done this yet, that's correct. But am I wrong?

62238169
Lol, lmao.

Congrats, boomers. You absolute champions of panic selling. How cooked are the investment firms that bought the top that will rush out the burning theatre frontrunning?

Get fucked, cunts. Your excess greed is your downfall. Kek, baggies.

62238202
>>62238169
Stay side lined trip faggot. No one asked for your reddit blog post opinion.

62238232
>>62237297
New builds (units especially) are an absolute trap that you shouldn't touch, the moment you buy one it immediately loses value because the next buyer doesn't get any of the same benefits
Build quality is shit, yield is shit, CG growth probably shit, risk is high
Established unit with high yields (what I was aiming for anyway) are probably going to be the better option, NG will be negligible and losses can be carried forward anyway

62238233
>>62223850
>x is fucked
>z is doomed
Kill yourself, mongo.

62238252
Any serious property discussion groups? Facebook? Reddit is useless and I dont know what's going on here but you're either twelve or indians

>>62238068
Yes. You're not going to accomplosh anything im the middle of nowhere. Unless you plan to become a monk.

>>62238232
Thoughts on apartment bhilt in 2020s?
>still under warranty
>currently very cheap
>2019 onwards government started cracking down on dodgy builds so new ones are supposed to be better

62238257
>>62237955
>You also dont pay stamp duty when you sell.
you gotta live somewhere
but yeah you right im retarded

62238274
>>62238252
>Any serious property discussion groups? Facebook? Reddit is useless and I dont know what's going on here but you're either twelve or indians
Propertychat
>Thoughts on apartment bhilt in 2020s?
Still think they're pretty shit and "government crackdowns" do nothing because it means they'd have to actually punish the browns that rort the system and Labor will never ever ever do that
Take everything I say with a grain of salt though, I don't actually have an IP yet
But I'm still aiming for high yield 60/70s apartments in outer suburbs that are primed for CG growth in the next few years (i.e. where FHBs are going after being priced out of the inner suburbs)

62238289
>>62238252
>>62238274
+ another point for units is that Australia is increasingly full of singles that have no use (let alone money) for a single family home
Rents also have a lot more room to grow despite how much doom and gloom you see surrounding it

item.title: The LINK fallacy — 7 replies — 9.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
The LINK fallacy

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-16 11:54 UTC

OP

Lots of bullishness on crypto twitter around Chainlink being the safe haven after the LayerZero hack and that's fine and all but there's one huge fallacy.

Yes, chains/protocols/apps have a reason to use Chainlink. But that's not a reason for investors to buy LINK.

Chainlink loves to talk about transaction volume, TVL, TVE, whatever the fuck. But they never actually disclose any fees. Except for a few exceptions where you literally cannot hide it, most of these deals are literal paper contracts between one corporate entity and Chainlink Labs. There is absolutely zero disclosure about the nature of those deals, how much money is actually flowing, what the length of the contracts are, if there is any money flowing or if it's just another BUILD grift where Chainlink gets 1% of some scam token supply at a date when all insiders and VCs have had the opportunity to dump.

Why the fuck did the Reserve revenue for example go down? >muh price go up
No the dollar value has literally decreased quite substantially. All these new "deals" and commitments but the reserve is just a weekly 1 million buyback from the several 100s millions of quarterly dumps Sergey makes. Staking is just the simplest, clunkiest fake yield farm you can make, completely disconnected from the Chainlink network and its services. And there is absolutely no disclosure of any plans to change any of that and there hasn't been for 4 years.

Early replies (first 3)

62238602
>>62238586
Basically I'm just not gonna sell them (my LINK tokens!!) I know.... UGH I know..... I'm sorry It's just that I'm not gonna sell them is all

62238603
These FUD talking points would make sense if XRP wasnt 10x mcap of LINK

NS

62238654
>>62238603
Rent free
Nice flippening

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62238673
>>62238586
All midcurve talking points. If crypto ever has another bullrun Link will 20x in a week and every KOL on twitter will be posting about how they’ve always owned it and it was the most obvious thing ever and this cycle is all about the institutional narrative and Link owns that narrative and if you didn’t buy you must have been living under a rock etc etc etc… so unless you want a lecture from smug Raoul Pal, Cobie and Carl from the Moon telling you how they always knew this would happen eventually, I’d suggest you get your fucking bags packed because that would be a fate worse than death if you’d spent the last five years poring over every announcement chainlink ever made.

This is my fundamental investment thesis btw

62238683
>>62238602
>>62238673
I'm selling all my unstaked linkies at 15 usd, i gave up brother. I also gave up on BTC, it's boomer etfs from now on, they have won. It's over

62238707
>>62238602
that's a good idea. Chainlink is needed for the new wall street

62238759
>>62238673
> If crypto ever has another bullrun Link will 20x
>This is my fundamental investment thesis btw

Pipe dreams of a baggie that believes in fundamentals in crypto, where meme coins such as Doge, Pepe, Shiba and other shitcoins exist.

Sell your bags before it drops to $5 and you kys, or just kys.

Btw, token not sneeded.

62238708

62236282

62236287

62236288
>>62236216
I want to see this fat scammer get rekted irl, then finally, I'm sure we'll pump to 1000

62236297
>>62236216
True. We're like shareholders without the rights or due diligence owed to shareholders, but with all of the capital benefits for the company. Cll keeps spouting truth>trust while leaving us completely in the dark on their financials. Enough is enough.

62236299
>>62236216
There is literally zero chance tokens have any value or carry over to the IPO.

62236304
>>62236288
Sergey has overflowing sex appeal. We need him alive.

62236339
Yeah but im just not gonna sell my totally useless tokens

62236354
>>62236297
>Cll keeps spouting truth>trust
There is no objective truth, only an approximation of truth. Sergey knows this acutely as a philosophy graduate. All human made systems are fallible.

62236400
How to protect against sybil/51% attacks?

Eventually chainlink will become permissionless. Then it will be subverted by nation states with unlimited funds. Sort of need the token.

62236406
>>62236297
>Cll keeps spouting truth>trust


From the FBI website...


>Enron actively projected a strong public image of honesty, integrity, and transparency while simultaneously using complex partnerships to hide billions in debt and manipulate profits


>Code of Ethics: Enron published and heavily promoted a formal corporate Code of Ethics. Signed by then-CEO Kenneth Lay, the foreword boasted that the company "enjoys a reputation for fairness and honesty".

>Core Values: Enron consistently listed integrity, respect, and communication as its foundational corporate values.


>Public Relations: The company frequently issued press releases and marketing campaigns that positioned itself as an ethical industry leader and a pioneer of the "new economy". It was even named Fortune's "Most Innovative Company in America" for six consecutive years leading up to its collapse


any of this feel familiar boys?

62236528
>>62236406
no, link becoming $81k was promised to me 3000 years ago

62236567
>>62236297
What do you mean enough is enough? You said it yourself, you have zero rights. Sergey owes you nothing

62236740
>>62236216

Kek

62236815
>>62236299
yeah what is governance or voting anyway?

>>62236234
Wait what FUD is it? it either makes nothing or all the deals are off-chain so we get nothing? I've lost track.

>>62236216
Almost like that was the plan all along, a lasting LEGACY.........

62236817
>>62236216
companies have earnings lmao

62236818
>>62236406
>emotionally charged rhetoric
There is no structural similarity to your argument. Comparing a tech company building infrastructure to a publicly traded energy trading conglomerate. Every company in the world uses words like
>trust
>integrity
>transparency
There are legitimate angles to criticize Chainlink but you seem incapable of doing that

62237191
>>62236276
I bought more today too

62237265

62238378

item.title: The BIGGEST Crypto Integration in History — 81 replies — 4.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
The BIGGEST Crypto Integration in History

Open thread • Replies: 81 • Updated: 2026-05-15 18:55 UTC

OP

Historic moment as Chainlink services have been selected to be used by the DTCC! This is monumental to the crypto and blockchain industry. They even pinned the tweet! Wow, to think how far we have come as an industry.

Early replies (first 3)

62224616
Is this good or bad?

62224627
when are people going to wake up and realize that chainlink is the only crypto that matters? when will the fucking price finally go up?

62224642
>>62224627
I think you honestly really needed the spoon feeding from autists here back in 2017 to gather the confidence we have in Link. By the time Link got on the crypto map in 2020 or whenever people were cemented in their cliques for whatever coin they believed in. Most of the crypto space are normalfaggots. I’ve gotten a handful of people to buy, but you can never tell them to go all in cuz they are not emotionally invested in link. I got6 people to invest 300, 300, 700, and 500 and 400 and 1700. Almost all of them

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62227473
>>62224616
bad, sell the news event + token not needed
>>62224627

>>62224974

>>62225125
yeah, as long as the tokens aren't needed you can have top of the NASDAQ sucking Chainlinks dick, people just stopped caring, it's not 2017 anymore. Back then it would've sent LINk to triple digits, but those days are long gone, peoeple wanna see actual demand nowadays, but it ain't coming, because Chainlink /= LINK

62227498
>>62224863

62227508

jesus dude that's like $10k, even if LINK does a 3x (and that's being generous) you'd only have $30k, what are you gonna do with that? lol

>>62229154

62229158
>>62229156

62229164

>>62229164

62232247

62232345
>>62227563
lmao
lmao
lmao
lmao

62232462
>>62232345
Swift, DTCC, Fidelity plus the other 600 more.

62232545
>>62224577

62232606
>>62232545
because when it hit $50 everything else in crypto was pumping for no reason at all, leading to a terrible crash, and the market has basically been dead for 6 years, with only bitcoin seeing consistent gains and solana's shitcoin casino being lamer and scammier than eth's. When accounting for inflation, this entire market has been on life support since 2021.

62232658
I'm so excited for you crazy kids

62232694
>DTCC settles $2.5 quadrillion per year
>Chainlink gets used for 1% of that
>1% of the settled value as collateral
>Full dilution
>1 LINK = $250

62232746
>>62232545

62232794
>>62232545
the fat fuck scammer has released like 400 million coins onto the market since then, diluting your bags. You got scammed.

62232796
>>62226079
I’m 45 next week

62232803
>>62232694
You factor swift and every single other company ever and plus front running and suddenly the meme $81,000 number doesn’t seem very meme.

62232805
>>62227397
Chainlink gatekeeps itself. I’ve told countless normies about a link in 8 years and about 6 people have bought in. And all but 1 have less than 1,000 and the other has 2,000. I also told some kid when playing poker and he got like half his frat to buy in. The people who I have told who didn’t buy wouldn’t buy anyway.

And anyone who does buy isn’t gonna buy anything more than a sui stack at most because you have to be emotionally invested in any investment in order to buy a lot. I have a very affable personality and energy and even tho I’m totally dumb on anything technical…. People believe me. Oh I also got a chick to buy 3,000 link during covid who has a husband.

62234063
>>62232794
Which is less than most.
Bitcoin for instance released half its supply by 2013-2014, which is also when it pumped the most.

In fact, all cryptos in general pumped the hardest when they inflated the hardest.

62234092
Chainlink is literally a cuckold club full of incels waiting for their "real life to begin". They've been bagholding for 9 years while Sergey (their God) dumped on them.

THE cuckolds of crypto

62234112
>>62224577
No greater fools left i see.

62236050
>>62232694
>MOAC
please give me what ever copium you are taking

62236496
duuuuuude don't question the cult
duuuuuuude this investment is for geniuses only
duuuuuuude partnerships


>Despite its internal culture of obfuscation, Enron projected an illusion of transparency, largely through a sophisticated public relations strategy. The company's charismatic leaders, Ken Lay and Jeff Skilling, were often seen on financial networks and at industry conferences, extolling Enron's virtues and its role as a market innovator.

>The company's annual reports and press releases were masterclasses in euphemism and grandiosity. Technical jargon and ambiguous language created an aura of complexity that dissuaded scrutiny. Questions were deflected with a common refrain: Enron was too innovative to be understood by the uninitiated.

62236514
>>62232694
>duuuuuude if we just capture 1%

you need to watch this https://www.youtube.com/shorts/7Z4-WRmZ8j8

OP

>never had a gf
>never been on a date
>now 36
>had jobs
>go to gym
>finally lose weight and have a BMI less than 25
>make multiple dating profiles on different apps
>zero matches

So I'm a confirmed subhuman. And, judging by the responses to ugly males who post their pics on Reddit and ask why they get no matches, normies openly laugh at me and think I deserve a low status life of slavery.

Why should I give a fuck about putting in effort at work or saving the west or whatever? Any sort of participation is a scam for someone like me.

>>62231869
no shit dumbass. if youve had meaningless sex with 50 women you've fried your brain romantically

62233012
kill fat people

62233014
bro online dating is HORRIBLE. Unless you're like the top 5% of hot guys out there it's gonna be a depressing humiliation ritual. You prob have to hire a professional photographer and make it look like you have a vibrant, exciting life with a bunch of staged pics. I used shitty selfies a decade ago, but now it doesnt cut it.

62233017
>>62233014
this. If not a chad you need to be fully optimized with pics and lifestyle. Otherwise irl, which probably means 1 girl opportunity every few months otherwise you spoil the well

62233024
>>62232652
This
It’s sickening once you look into it.
Women at large are getting blamed when half the women on there are fake profiles anyway. There’s plenty of fake men’s profiles now too, dedicated to romance scams. Which is a whole other issue, men and women being victims

62233040
>>62233017
>that ugly guy with super polished tryhard profile

this unironically gives girls the ick

62233064
>>62233040
they get the ick from the guy being ugly. Selecting photos where he looks most attractive is only going to help compared to bathroom selfies. If he does that he'll get 0 matches guaranteed.

62233065
>>62233040
If hes ugly they already have the ick

62233066
>>62233065
exactly. The most important tip is don't be ugly. 2nd is don't be autistic as fuck. 3rd actually talk to girls whether online or irl whatever.

62233115
>>62223664
Dating apps arent for dating they're for sluts to get their holes blown out by a small selection of chads and fuckbois.

62233200
>>62233115
>chads
>fuckbois
what's the difference?

62233241
>>62233200
Chads don't have to try because their appearance does everything. Fuckbois have to try hard to get women to fuck them. They still have fuck but the level of effort is completely different.

62233271
>>62223664
I posted my face on soc once when I was drunk.

Everyone gave me an 7 or 8/10. I still feel ugly though.

62233283
>>62231723
5/10 roasties only swipe on 8+

62233302
>>62233283
/thread

All women swipe 3+ points up. If you're only attracting morbidly obese women, down syndrome women and old fat single mothers, you're an above average looking guy.

These studies have already been done 1,000 times. Average looking guys get 1 match a month abs it's usually a fake/bot account or a 300+ pound clown with 20 fishhooks in its face.

62233310
>>62223664
your ugly ancestors got pussy somehow and so can you

62233355
>>62223664
Remember, for every beautiful person, there must be 5 ugly ones.

I don't make the rules.

62233408
>>62223664
Why do you want a girlfriend? If you want to LOVE a woman you are gay, sorry to tell you. You need to start niggermaxxing, learn to pump and dump

62233456
>>62223664
Go to Philippines, Moldova, or Gambia, depending on what race you prefer. You'll get more girlfriends than you know what to do with.

I'd post some pix of hot bare-titties Himba girls but the jannies get all assblasted about that for some reason.

62233560
>>62232980
>Where in person did you meet your wife?
GF of a friend introduced me to her.
This strategy requires having friends, I know that's too much to ask of GenZ nowadays.

62233715
>>62233271
i never posted on soc but i did on the site photofeeler where people rate your pic. Out of 120 rates i averaged to a 3/10

which makes sense since im khv at 30. I am way too scared to post my face on this site though thats crazy

62233733
you can't be this naive at 36, its surprising how many manchildren there are in the west still chasing dating and love and marriage when your zoomer generation have figured it out for the scam that it is

62233792
>>62233733
yep, one of the most popular streamers smashes his face with a hammer... lmfao

all of this was figured out over a decade ago but no one wanted to listen.

62234041
>>62232735
>social pariah wonders why he's not surrounded by a harem of qt3.14s
many such cases

62234043
>>62233715
I'm 32 and a virgin. Just dick in pussy/ass virgin. I've done everything else. Also 6'3" and slender/fit.

62234052
Maybe you shouldn't spend so much time around permavirgins (reddit)

62234972
>>62233241
oh, I guess I'm a fuckboi. I've optimized everything possible and fuck a lot. I also live in a small town and have chicks drive 70 miles to my place on the regular for a first meetup. Got one tonight and another next Thurs for a "sleepover"

>>62233283
They do but eventually they run out of those dudes or they don't followup with meeting up. Sub8s wont see the chicks who just signed up for the apps but they'll likely see her after a few months. She'll humble herself after these dudes just pump and dump them over and over and they start going for the charming less attractive guy who ultimately just fucks and dumps then anyway, then they go for true beta bucks after that.

>>62233310
there werent many options back then and girls needed a guy for survival. Now every girl has the world at her fingertips and their own money.

>>62233456
yea this is the only option for a sub5 guy or mgtow

>>62233560
Yea your way is the best. I had a few of those situation but I never liked the girl enough or she had to move for college etc unfortunately. It really comes down to luck.

>>62233733
>chasing dating and love
this was exciting when I was a teenager. As an adult it's only fun to pump and dump. I do think love can exist if you find a good woman to build a family with though.

62235106
>>62223664
Bro stop using apps. Also those things you listed are no substitute for being able to competently speak to and entertain women. It is, unfortunately, a learned skill. You have to practice. A good way to learn is to flirt with women a little older than you. They typically like the attention from a younger man and are more forgiving of missteps.

62235448
Being ugly doesn't matter. Being 36 doesn't matter. Focus on confidence, charisma and making women feel heard and seen. Don't put so much weight on it, be a fun person to be around. Play around and test things out. You do really make your own reality. Don't convince yourself of these negative things.

62236519
>>62223664
>1pbtid demoralization
>>/glownigger/

item.title: remember when LINK cucks spent the entirety of 2022 — 19 replies — 3.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
remember when LINK cucks spent the entirety of 2022

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-05-16 07:22 UTC

OP

blaming Bulgarians and NEXO for their shitty price performance while calling anyone who made fun of their bags Bulgarians

Early replies (first 3)

62236293
>>62236269
kek fuddie there was literal proof

62236306
>>62236293
are Bulgarians the reason you're still a poor baggie in 2026? lol

62236412
>>62236269
LOL

i remember repeatedly being called bulgarian back then

then when that wore off there was the guy who always posted the na pidgeon language or whatever implying i was african or something. never really understood that one

and on and on it goes... the cult always has a mythological bad guy who seemingly has so much power he can suppress their billion dollar asset indefinitely

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62236572
>>62236412
hahahahahaha I remember that too. There was a schizo Linkie calling people Nigerians. The cherry on top of the cake is that Chainlink panders to niggers and even pays them to participate in crypto conferences

62236597
It's always one more integration until moon.
One more partnership until moon.
One more law passed until moon.
The latest carrot on a stick is the last one. For real this time.
This year is the year. All the breadcrumbs are fulfilled. Or something. Any pattern recognition is just "fud."

62236602
>>62236293
No one needs to fud LINK, sweatie.

62236652
>>62236269
Why even make fun of it anymore? This project has gone nowhere for almost 5 years maybe longer, at this point you're giving this dead project more attention than it deserves

62237093
>>62236652
OP and everyone ITT holds LINK

62237126
>>62237093
Baggie cannot envision anyone not holding LINK (also can't rotate an apple in his head or tell you how he would feel if he'd skipped breakfast this morning)

62237134
i remember being called a bulgarian and it was crazy funny because im actually a bulgarian living in bulgaria

62237144
>>62236269
Cripple

62237499
>>62236269
He unironically won. He shorted link from the top while dumping deposited tokens at nexo and made millions.

62237634
>>62236269
Hey hey hey, I am Bulgarian.

62237906
just admit you lost the game of life, stinkies
admit this and i will recommend to the machine elves to wake you from the simulation

62237923
>>62236269
>>62236412
>>62236652
>>62237134
>>62237499
>>62237906
yikes

62237998
I wish I was Bulgarian

62238007
>>62237998
me 2

62238043
>>62237923
P+C

62238336
>>62236597
seethe Bulgarian fuddie, once Clarity Act passes Chainlink will pump to $11

62235170

62235227
